tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Urine is cloudy, brown, with protein, glucose, bilirubin, and ketones present, but no nitrites or leukocytes, making infection less likely. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and possible underlying causes.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
